128	  buz_enroll RBZ_REKON_RELIQUARY        "rbw-irr" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_rekon_reliquary"       "param1"  "List tool images present under a reliquary stamp's GAR subtree"
129	  buz_enroll RBZ_AUDIT_HALLMARKS        "rbw-iah" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_audit_hallmarks"       ""  "Audit hallmarks — list all hallmark identifiers"
130	  buz_enroll RBZ_AUDIT_RELIQUARIES      "rbw-iar" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_audit_reliquaries"     ""  "Audit reliquaries — list all reliquary stamps"
131	  buz_enroll RBZ_AUDIT_ENSHRINEMENTS    "rbw-iae" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_audit_enshrinements"   ""  "Audit enshrinements — list all enshrined base anchors"
132	  buz_enroll RBZ_WREST_HALLMARK_IMAGE   "rbw-iwh" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_wrest"                 "param1"  "Wrest a hallmark image from registry"
133	  buz_enroll RBZ_WREST_RELIQUARY_IMAGE  "rbw-iwr" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_wrest"                 "param1"  "Wrest a reliquary tool image from registry"
134	  buz_enroll RBZ_WREST_ENSHRINED_IMAGE  "rbw-iwe" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_wrest"                 "param1"  "Wrest an enshrined base image from registry"
135	  buz_enroll RBZ_JETTISON_HALLMARK_IMAGE  "rbw-iJh" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_jettison"            "param1"  "Jettison a hallmark image tag from registry"
136	  buz_enroll RBZ_JETTISON_RELIQUARY_IMAGE "rbw-iJr" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_jettison"            "param1"  "Jettison a reliquary tool image from registry"
137	  buz_enroll RBZ_JETTISON_ENSHRINEMENT    "rbw-iJe" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_jettison"            "param1"  "Jettison an enshrinement from registry"
138	
139	  # Marshal — lifecycle (rbw-M)
140	  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_MARSHAL    "rbw-M"   "Marshal — Lifecycle"
141	  buz_enroll RBZ_MARSHAL_ZERO           "rbw-MZ"  "rblm_cli.sh" "rblm_zero"      ""  "Zero regime to blank template"
142	  buz_enroll RBZ_MARSHAL_PROOF          "rbw-MP"  "rblm_cli.sh" "rblm_proof"     ""  "Proof repo for release testing"
143	  buz_enroll RBZ_MARSHAL_GENERATE       "rbw-MG"  "rblm_cli.sh" "rblm_generate"  ""  "Generate tabtarget context from zipper registry"
144	
145	  # Nameplate — cross-nameplate operations (rbw-n)
146	  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_NAMEPLATE  "rbw-n"   "Nameplate — Cross-nameplate operations"
147	  z_mod="rbrn_cli.sh"
148	  buz_enroll RBZ_LIST_NAMEPLATES        "rbw-rnl" "${z_mod}" "rbrn_list"    ""  "List all nameplates"
149	  buz_enroll RBZ_SURVEY_NAMEPLATES      "rbw-ni"  "${z_mod}" "rbrn_survey"  ""  "Survey nameplate status"
150	  buz_enroll RBZ_AUDIT_NAMEPLATES       "rbw-nv"  "${z_mod}" "rbrn_audit"   ""  "Validate all nameplates"
151	
152	  # Regime — config files (rbw-r)
153	  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_REGIME     "rbw-r"   "Regime — Config files"
154	  z_mod="rbrn_cli.sh"
155	  buz_enroll RBZ_RENDER_NAMEPLATE       "rbw-rnr" "${z_mod}" "rbrn_render"    "param1"  "Render nameplate regime"
156	  buz_enroll RBZ_VALIDATE_NAMEPLATE     "rbw-rnv" "${z_mod}" "rbrn_validate"  "param1"  "Validate nameplate regime"
157	  z_mod="rbrv_cli.sh"
158	  buz_enroll RBZ_LIST_VESSELS           "rbw-rvl" "${z_mod}" "rbrv_list"      ""        "List vessel regimes"
159	  buz_enroll RBZ_RENDER_VESSEL          "rbw-rvr" "${z_mod}" "rbrv_render"    "param1"  "Render vessel regime"
160	  buz_enroll RBZ_VALIDATE_VESSEL        "rbw-rvv" "${z_mod}" "rbrv_validate"  "param1"  "Validate vessel regime"
161	  z_mod="rbrr_cli.sh"
162	  buz_enroll RBZ_RENDER_REPO            "rbw-rrr" "${z_mod}" "rbrr_render"    ""  "Render repo regime"
163	  buz_enroll RBZ_VALIDATE_REPO          "rbw-rrv" "${z_mod}" "rbrr_validate"  ""  "Validate repo regime"
164	  z_mod="rbrp_cli.sh"
165	  buz_enroll RBZ_RENDER_PAYOR           "rbw-rpr" "${z_mod}" "rbrp_render"    ""  "Render payor regime"
166	  buz_enroll RBZ_VALIDATE_PAYOR         "rbw-rpv" "${z_mod}" "rbrp_validate"  ""  "Validate payor regime"
167	  z_mod="rbro_cli.sh"
168	  buz_enroll RBZ_RENDER_OAUTH           "rbw-ror" "${z_mod}" "rbro_render"    ""  "Render OAuth regime"
169	  buz_enroll RBZ_VALIDATE_OAUTH         "rbw-rov" "${z_mod}" "rbro_validate"  ""  "Validate OAuth regime"
170	  z_mod="rbrs_cli.sh"
171	  buz_enroll RBZ_RENDER_STATION         "rbw-rsr" "${z_mod}" "rbrs_render"    ""  "Render station regime"
172	  buz_enroll RBZ_VALIDATE_STATION       "rbw-rsv" "${z_mod}" "rbrs_validate"  ""  "Validate station regime"
173	  z_mod="rbra_cli.sh"
174	  buz_enroll RBZ_RENDER_AUTH            "rbw-rar" "${z_mod}" "rbra_render"    "param1"  "Render auth regime"
175	  buz_enroll RBZ_VALIDATE_AUTH          "rbw-rav" "${z_mod}" "rbra_validate"  "param1"  "Validate auth regime"
176	  buz_enroll RBZ_LIST_AUTH              "rbw-ral" "${z_mod}" "rbra_list"      ""        "List auth regimes"
177	
178	  # Theurge — test infrastructure (rbw-t)
179	  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_THEURGE    "rbw-t"   "Theurge — Test infrastructure"
180	  z_mod="rbob_cli.sh"
181	  buz_enroll RBZ_THEURGE_KLUDGE  "rbw-tK"  "${z_mod}" "rbob_kludge"        "imprint"  "Local kludge build + install hallmark into nameplate"
182	  buz_enroll RBZ_THEURGE_ORDAIN  "rbw-tO"  "${z_mod}" "rbob_ordain"        "imprint"  "Cloud build + install hallmark into nameplate"
183	  z_mod="rbq_cli.sh"
184	  buz_enroll RBZ_QUALIFY_FAST     "rbw-tf"   "${z_mod}" "rbq_qualify_fast"     ""        "Fast qualify: tabtargets, colophons, nameplate health"
185	  buz_enroll RBZ_QUALIFY_RELEASE  "rbw-tr"   "${z_mod}" "rbq_qualify_release"  ""        "Release qualify: + shellcheck, full test suite"
186	  buz_enroll RBZ_QUALIFY_PRISTINE "rbw-tP"   "${z_mod}" "rbq_qualify_pristine" ""        "Pristine qualify: gauntlet test suite (release gate)"
187	
188	  # Handbook — human-facing procedures (rbw-h0 index, rbw-hw/HW* windows)
189	  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_HANDBOOK   "rbw-HW"  "Handbook — Human-facing procedures"
190	  z_mod="rbh0/rbhw0_cli.sh"
191	  buz_enroll RBZ_HANDBOOK_TOP           "rbw-h0"    "${z_mod}" "rbhw_handbook_top"              ""        "Top-level handbook index across all groups"
192	  buz_enroll RBZ_HANDBOOK_WINDOWS       "rbw-hw"    "${z_mod}" "rbhw_top"                       ""        "Windows test infrastructure orchestrator"
193	  buz_enroll RBZ_HW_DOCKER_DESKTOP     "rbw-HWdd"  "${z_mod}" "rbhw_docker_desktop"            ""        "Docker Desktop install"
194	  buz_enroll RBZ_HW_DOCKER_CONTEXT     "rbw-HWdc"  "${z_mod}" "rbhw_docker_context_discipline" ""        "Deterministic daemon selection"
195	
196	  readonly ZRBZ_COLOPHON_MANIFEST="${z_buz_colophon_roll[*]}"
197	
198	  readonly ZRBZ_KINDLED=1
199	}
200	
201	######################################################################
202	# Healthcheck (validates all enrolled tabtargets exist on disk)
203	
204	zrbz_healthcheck() {
205	  zrbz_sentinel
206	  buz_healthcheck
207	}
208	
209	######################################################################
210	# Internal sentinel
211	
212	zrbz_sentinel() {
213	  test "${ZRBZ_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbz not kindled - call zrbz_kindle first"
214	}
215	
216	# eof
217

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# RBW Zipper - Colophon registry for RBW workbench dispatch

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard
test -z "${ZRBZ_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZRBZ_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Colophon registry initialization

zrbz_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBZ_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "rbz already kindled"

  # Verify buz zipper is kindled (CLI furnish must kindle buz first)
  zbuz_sentinel

  # Accounts — Google Cloud service accounts (rbw-a)
  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_ACCOUNTS   "rbw-a"   "Accounts — Service account management"
  local z_mod="rbgp_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR         "rbw-aM"  "${z_mod}" "rbgp_governor_mantle"       ""        "Create/replace governor service account"
  z_mod="rbgg_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_INVEST_RETRIEVER        "rbw-arI" "${z_mod}" "rbgg_invest_retriever"      "param1"  "Invest a Retriever service account for an identity"
  buz_enroll RBZ_INVEST_DIRECTOR         "rbw-adI" "${z_mod}" "rbgg_invest_director"       "param1"  "Invest a Director service account for an identity"
  buz_enroll RBZ_DIVEST_RETRIEVER        "rbw-arD" "${z_mod}" "rbgg_divest_retriever"      "param1"  "Divest a Retriever service account by identity"
  buz_enroll RBZ_DIVEST_DIRECTOR         "rbw-adD" "${z_mod}" "rbgg_divest_director"       "param1"  "Divest a Director service account by identity"
  buz_enroll RBZ_ROSTER_RETRIEVERS       "rbw-arr" "${z_mod}" "rbgg_roster_retrievers"     ""        "Roster Retriever service accounts (emit per-identity fact files)"
  buz_enroll RBZ_ROSTER_DIRECTORS        "rbw-adr" "${z_mod}" "rbgg_roster_directors"      ""        "Roster Director service accounts (emit per-identity fact files)"

  # Crucible — container runtime (rbw-c)
  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_CRUCIBLE   "rbw-c"   "Crucible — Container runtime"
  z_mod="rbob_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_CRUCIBLE_CHARGE  "rbw-cC"  "${z_mod}" "rbob_charge"       "imprint"  "Charge crucible (sentry + pentacle + bottle containers)"
  buz_enroll RBZ_CRUCIBLE_QUENCH  "rbw-cQ"  "${z_mod}" "rbob_quench"       "imprint"  "Quench crucible"
  buz_enroll RBZ_CRUCIBLE_SSH     "rbw-cS"  "${z_mod}" "rbob_ssh"          "imprint"  "SSH into the bottle container"
  buz_enroll RBZ_CRUCIBLE_HAIL    "rbw-ch"  "${z_mod}" "rbob_hail"         "param1"   "Shell into the sentry container"
  buz_enroll RBZ_CRUCIBLE_RACK    "rbw-cr"  "${z_mod}" "rbob_rack"         "param1"   "Shell into the bottle container"
  buz_enroll RBZ_CRUCIBLE_SCRY    "rbw-cs"  "${z_mod}" "rbob_scry"         "param1"   "Observe network traffic on crucible containers"
  buz_enroll RBZ_CRUCIBLE_WRIT    "rbw-cw"  "${z_mod}" "rbob_writ"         "imprint"  "Non-interactive exec in sentry container"
  buz_enroll RBZ_CRUCIBLE_FIAT    "rbw-cf"  "${z_mod}" "rbob_fiat"         "imprint"  "Non-interactive exec in pentacle container"
  buz_enroll RBZ_CRUCIBLE_BARK    "rbw-cb"  "${z_mod}" "rbob_bark"         "imprint"  "Non-interactive exec in bottle container"
  buz_enroll RBZ_CRUCIBLE_ACTIVE  "rbw-cic" "${z_mod}" "rbob_charged"      "param1"   "Check if crucible is currently charged"
  buz_enroll RBZ_CRUCIBLE_KLUDGE_BOTTLE "rbw-cKB" "${z_mod}" "rbob_kludge_bottle" "param1" "Kludge bottle vessel and drive hallmark into nameplate"
  buz_enroll RBZ_CRUCIBLE_KLUDGE_SENTRY "rbw-cKS" "${z_mod}" "rbob_kludge_sentry" "param1" "Kludge sentry vessel and drive hallmark into nameplate"

  # Depot — GCP project infrastructure (rbw-d, UPPER=mutates, lower=read)
  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_DEPOT      "rbw-d"   "Depot — GCP project infrastructure"
  z_mod="rbgp_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_LEVY_DEPOT             "rbw-dL"  "${z_mod}" "rbgp_depot_levy"   ""  "Provision GCP depot project"
  buz_enroll RBZ_UNMAKE_DEPOT           "rbw-dU"  "${z_mod}" "rbgp_depot_unmake"  "param1"  "Permanently remove a depot (operator supplies depot project ID)"
  buz_enroll RBZ_LIST_DEPOT             "rbw-dl"  "${z_mod}" "rbgp_depot_list"    ""  "List all active depots"
  z_mod="rbfd_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_ENSHRINE_VESSEL        "rbw-dE"  "${z_mod}" "rbfd_enshrine"      "param1"  "Enshrine upstream base images to GAR via Cloud Build"
  z_mod="rbfl_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_INSCRIBE_RELIQUARY     "rbw-dI"  "${z_mod}" "rbfl_inscribe"      ""  "Create reliquary: mirror tool images from upstream to GAR"
  buz_enroll RBZ_YOKE_RELIQUARY         "rbw-dY"  "${z_mod}" "rbfl_yoke"          "param1"  "Yoke a reliquary stamp into every vessel's rbrv.env (wildcard fan-out)"

  # Guide — human-directed procedures (rbw-g)

local z_sigils=""
  z_sigils=$(rbrv_list_capture) || buc_die "No vessels found"
  buc_step "Available vessels:"
  local z_s=""
  for z_s in ${z_sigils}; do
    buc_bare "        ${z_s}"
  done
  if test -z "${z_sigil}"; then
    buc_die "Vessel parameter required"
  fi
  buc_die "Vessel not found: ${z_sigil}"
}

# Resolve vessel argument: accepts a sigil (e.g., rbev-sentry-deb-tether) or a path
# (e.g., rbev-vessels/rbev-sentry-deb-tether).  On no-arg or invalid arg, lists
# available vessels and dies.  On success, writes resolved path to ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE.
zrbfc_resolve_vessel() {
  zrbfc_sentinel

  local -r z_arg="${1:-}"

  # Try as path first, then as sigil under RBRR_VESSEL_DIR
  if test -n "${z_arg}" && test -d "${z_arg}" && test -f "${z_arg}/rbrv.env"; then
    printf '%s' "${z_arg}" > "${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to write resolved vessel path"
    return 0
  fi
  if test -n "${z_arg}" && test -d "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_arg}" && test -f "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_arg}/rbrv.env"; then
    printf '%s' "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_arg}" > "${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to write resolved vessel path"
    return 0
  fi

  # Resolution failed — list available vessels and die
  local z_sigils=""
  z_sigils=$(rbrv_list_capture) || buc_die "No vessels found"
  buc_step "Available vessels:"
  local z_sigil=""
  for z_sigil in ${z_sigils}; do
    buc_bare "        ${z_sigil}"
  done
  if test -z "${z_arg}"; then
    buc_die "Vessel argument required (sigil or path)"
  fi
  buc_die "Vessel not found: ${z_arg}"
}

zrbfc_load_vessel() {
  zrbfc_sentinel

  local z_vessel_dir="$1"

  buc_log_args 'Validate vessel directory exists'
  test -d "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Vessel directory not found: ${z_vessel_dir}"

  buc_log_args 'Check for rbrv.env file'
  local z_vessel_env="${z_vessel_dir}/rbrv.env"
  test -f "${z_vessel_env}" || buc_die "Vessel configuration not found: ${z_vessel_env}"

  buc_log_args 'Source vessel configuration'

# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

zrbfk_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TOOLS_DIR        " "Project tools root directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TEMP_DIR         " "Bash Dispatch Utility provided temporary directory, empty at start of command"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_NOW_STAMP        " "Bash Dispatch Utility provided string unique between invocations"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  source "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/rbbc_constants.sh"
  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buf_fact.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbcc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgl_GarLayout.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgd_DepotConstants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrr_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh"
  source "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgo_OAuth.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgu_Utility.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbfk_kludge.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbz_zipper.sh"

  zbuv_kindle

  buc_log_args 'Validate BUD environment'
  zburd_kindle

  buc_log_args 'Validate required tools'
  command -v git >/dev/null 2>&1 || buc_die "git not found - required for assuring controlled build context"

  buc_log_args 'Container runtime settings'
  RBG_RUNTIME="${RBG_RUNTIME:-podman}"
  RBG_RUNTIME_ARG="${RBG_RUNTIME_ARG:-}"

  zrbcc_kindle

  zrbrr_kindle
  zrbrr_enforce
  zrbdc_kindle

  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrv_regime.sh"

  buc_log_args 'Kindle modules in dependency order'
  zrbgc_kindle
  zrbgl_kindle
  zrbgd_kindle
  zrbgo_kindle
  zrbgu_kindle
  zrbfh_kindle
  zrbfk_kindle

  zbuz_kindle
  zrbz_kindle
}

buc_execute rbfk_ "Recipe Bottle Foundry Kludge" zrbfk_furnish "$@"

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle Foundry Core - shared infrastructure for all Foundry child modules
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	# Multiple inclusion guard (silent skip — rbfc is sourced by multiple child modules)
24	test -z "${ZRBFC_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
25	ZRBFC_SOURCED=1
26	
27	######################################################################
28	# Internal Functions (zrbfc_*)
29	
30	zrbfc_kindle() {
31	  test -z "${ZRBFC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfc already kindled"
32	
33	  # Validate environment
34	  zburd_sentinel
35	
36	  buc_log_args 'Check required GCB/GAR environment variables'
37	  zrbgc_sentinel
38	
39	  buc_log_args 'Module Variables (ZRBFC_*)'
40	  readonly ZRBFC_GCB_API_BASE="https://cloudbuild.googleapis.com/v1"
41	  readonly ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE="https://artifactregistry.googleapis.com/v1"
42	  readonly ZRBFC_CLOUD_QUERY_BASE="https://console.cloud.google.com/cloud-build/builds"
43	
44	  readonly ZRBFC_GCB_PROJECT_BUILDS_URL="${ZRBFC_GCB_API_BASE}/projects/${RBGD_GCB_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${RBGD_GCB_REGION}/builds"
45	  readonly ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE="projects/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}/repositories/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
46	
47	  buc_log_args 'Trigger dispatch endpoints'
48	  readonly ZRBFC_TRIGGERS_URL="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBUILD}${RBGC_CLOUDBUILD_V1}/projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${RBGD_GCB_REGION}/triggers"
49	
50	  buc_log_args 'Registry API endpoints'
51	  readonly ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
52	  readonly ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH="${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
53	  readonly ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE="https://${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/v2/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}"
54	
55	  buc_log_args 'Media types for registry operations'
56	  readonly ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES="application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json,application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json,application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json,application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json"
57	
58	  buc_log_args 'Define build polling files'
59	  readonly ZRBFC_BUILD_ID_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_build_id.txt"
60	  readonly ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_build_status.json"
61	  readonly ZRBFC_BUILD_START_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_build_start.txt"
62	  readonly ZRBFC_BUILD_FINISH_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_build_finish.txt"
63	
64	  buc_log_args 'Per-poll-attempt temp file prefixes (suffixed by poll counter for forensics)'
65	  readonly ZRBFC_POLL_RESPONSE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_poll_response_"
66	  readonly ZRBFC_POLL_CODE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_poll_code_"
67	  readonly ZRBFC_POLL_STDERR_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_poll_stderr_"
68	  readonly ZRBFC_POLL_ERR_CHECK_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_poll_err_check_"
69	  readonly ZRBFC_POLL_STATUS_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_poll_status_"
70	
71	  buc_log_args 'Define git info file (used by stitch)'
72	  readonly ZRBFC_GIT_INFO_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_git_info.json"
73	
74	  buc_log_args 'Define git metadata files (shared across about/mirror submissions)'
75	  readonly ZRBFC_GIT_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_git_"
76	  readonly ZRBFC_GIT_COMMIT_FILE="${ZRBFC_GIT_PREFIX}commit.txt"
77	  readonly ZRBFC_GIT_BRANCH_FILE="${ZRBFC_GIT_PREFIX}branch.txt"
78	  readonly ZRBFC_GIT_REPO_FILE="${ZRBFC_GIT_PREFIX}repo.txt"
79	
80	  buc_log_args 'Vessel-related files'
81	  readonly ZRBFC_VESSEL_SIGIL_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_vessel_sigil.txt"
82	  readonly ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_vessel_resolved_dir.txt"
83	
84	  buc_log_args 'Define output files (BURD_OUTPUT_DIR — persists after dispatch)'
85	  readonly ZRBFC_OUTPUT_VESSEL_DIR="${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/rbfc_vessel_dir.txt"
86	
87	  buc_log_args 'Scratch file for sequential temp-file patterns'
88	  readonly ZRBFC_SCRATCH_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_scratch.txt"
89	
90	  buc_log_args 'GAR package enumeration output (zrbfc_list_packages_capture)'
91	  readonly ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_package_list.txt"
92	
93	  buc_log_args 'Step script directories (used by shared about/vouch/preflight assembly)'
94	  local z_self_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
95	  readonly ZRBFC_RBGJA_STEPS_DIR="${z_self_dir}/rbgja"
96	  test -d "${ZRBFC_RBGJA_STEPS_DIR}" || buc_die "RBGJA steps directory not found: ${ZRBFC_RBGJA_STEPS_DIR}"
97	  readonly ZRBFC_RBGJV_STEPS_DIR="${z_self_dir}/rbgjv"
98	  test -d "${ZRBFC_RBGJV_STEPS_DIR}" || buc_die "RBGJV steps directory not found: ${ZRBFC_RBGJV_STEPS_DIR}"
99	  readonly ZRBFC_RBGJR_STEPS_DIR="${z_self_dir}/rbgjr"
100	  test -d "${ZRBFC_RBGJR_STEPS_DIR}" || buc_die "RBGJR steps directory not found: ${ZRBFC_RBGJR_STEPS_DIR}"
101	
102	  buc_log_args 'Tool image refs — mutable kindle state, populated by zrbfc_resolve_tool_images'
103	  z_rbfc_tool_gcloud=""
104	  z_rbfc_tool_docker=""
105	  z_rbfc_tool_alpine=""
106	  z_rbfc_tool_syft=""
107	  z_rbfc_tool_binfmt=""
108	  z_rbfc_tool_skopeo=""
109	
110	  readonly ZRBFC_KINDLED=1
111	}
112	
113	zrbfc_sentinel() {
114	  test "${ZRBFC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbfc not kindled - call zrbfc_kindle first"
115	}
116	
117	# Internal: Resolve tool image references from reliquary.
118	# Must be called after vessel load (reads RBRV_RELIQUARY).
119	# Sets module-level z_rbfc_tool_* mutable kindle state for downstream step assembly.
120	# Idempotent — safe to call multiple times per invocation.
121	zrbfc_resolve_tool_images() {
122	  zrbfc_sentinel
123	
124	  local -r z_reliquary="${RBRV_RELIQUARY:-}"
125	  test -n "${z_reliquary}" \
126	    || buc_die "RBRV_RELIQUARY is required — run inscribe to create a reliquary first"
127	
128	  local -r z_rqy_prefix="${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}/${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/${z_reliquary}"
129	  z_rbfc_tool_gcloud="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_GCLOUD}:${z_reliquary}"
130	  z_rbfc_tool_docker="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_DOCKER}:${z_reliquary}"
131	  z_rbfc_tool_alpine="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_ALPINE}:${z_reliquary}"
132	  z_rbfc_tool_syft="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SYFT}:${z_reliquary}"
133	  z_rbfc_tool_binfmt="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_BINFMT}:${z_reliquary}"
134	  z_rbfc_tool_skopeo="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SKOPEO}:${z_reliquary}"
135	  buc_log_args "Tool images resolved from reliquary: ${z_reliquary}"
136	}
137	
138	# Validate that a vessel sigil is non-empty and corresponds to a known vessel.
139	# On missing or invalid sigil, lists available vessels and dies.
140	# Does NOT resolve to a directory — use zrbfc_resolve_vessel for that.
141	rbfc_require_vessel_sigil() {
142	  zrbfc_sentinel
143	
144	  local -r z_sigil="${1:-}"
145	
146	  if test -n "${z_sigil}" && test -d "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_sigil}" && test -f "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_sigil}/rbrv.env"; then
147	    return 0
148	  fi
149	
150	  local z_sigils=""

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard (silent skip — rbfc is sourced by multiple child modules)
test -z "${ZRBFC_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZRBFC_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbfc_*)

zrbfc_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBFC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfc already kindled"

  # Validate environment
  zburd_sentinel

  buc_log_args 'Check required GCB/GAR environment variables'
  zrbgc_sentinel

  buc_log_args 'Module Variables (ZRBFC_*)'
  readonly ZRBFC_GCB_API_BASE="https://cloudbuild.googleapis.com/v1"
  readonly ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE="https://artifactregistry.googleapis.com/v1"
  readonly ZRBFC_CLOUD_QUERY_BASE="https://console.cloud.google.com/cloud-build/builds"

  readonly ZRBFC_GCB_PROJECT_BUILDS_URL="${ZRBFC_GCB_API_BASE}/projects/${RBGD_GCB_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${RBGD_GCB_REGION}/builds"
  readonly ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE="projects/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}/repositories/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"

  buc_log_args 'Trigger dispatch endpoints'
  readonly ZRBFC_TRIGGERS_URL="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBUILD}${RBGC_CLOUDBUILD_V1}/projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${RBGD_GCB_REGION}/triggers"

  buc_log_args 'Registry API endpoints'
  readonly ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
  readonly ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH="${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
  readonly ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE="https://${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/v2/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}"

  buc_log_args 'Media types for registry operations'
  readonly ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES="application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json,application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json,application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json,application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json"

  buc_log_args 'Define build polling files'
  readonly ZRBFC_BUILD_ID_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_build_id.txt"
  readonly ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_build_status.json"
  readonly ZRBFC_BUILD_START_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_build_start.txt"
  readonly ZRBFC_BUILD_FINISH_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_build_finish.txt"

  buc_log_args 'Per-poll-attempt temp file prefixes (suffixed by poll counter for forensics)'
  readonly ZRBFC_POLL_RESPONSE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_poll_response_"
  readonly ZRBFC_POLL_CODE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_poll_code_"
  readonly ZRBFC_POLL_STDERR_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_poll_stderr_"
  readonly ZRBFC_POLL_ERR_CHECK_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_poll_err_check_"
  readonly ZRBFC_POLL_STATUS_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_poll_status_"

  buc_log_args 'Define git info file (used by stitch)'
  readonly ZRBFC_GIT_INFO_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_git_info.json"

  buc_log_args 'Define git metadata files (shared across about/mirror submissions)'

  readonly ZRBFC_GIT_COMMIT_FILE="${ZRBFC_GIT_PREFIX}commit.txt"
  readonly ZRBFC_GIT_BRANCH_FILE="${ZRBFC_GIT_PREFIX}branch.txt"
  readonly ZRBFC_GIT_REPO_FILE="${ZRBFC_GIT_PREFIX}repo.txt"

  buc_log_args 'Vessel-related files'

  readonly ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_vessel_resolved_dir.txt"

  buc_log_args 'Define output files (BURD_OUTPUT_DIR — persists after dispatch)'
  readonly ZRBFC_OUTPUT_VESSEL_DIR="${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/rbfc_vessel_dir.txt"

  buc_log_args 'Scratch file for sequential temp-file patterns'
  readonly ZRBFC_SCRATCH_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_scratch.txt"

  buc_log_args 'GAR package enumeration output (zrbfc_list_packages_capture)'
  readonly ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_package_list.txt"

  buc_log_args 'Step script directories (used by shared about/vouch/preflight assembly)'
  local z_self_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
  readonly ZRBFC_RBGJA_STEPS_DIR="${z_self_dir}/rbgja"
  test -d "${ZRBFC_RBGJA_STEPS_DIR}" || buc_die "RBGJA steps directory not found: ${ZRBFC_RBGJA_STEPS_DIR}"
  readonly ZRBFC_RBGJV_STEPS_DIR="${z_self_dir}/rbgjv"
  test -d "${ZRBFC_RBGJV_STEPS_DIR}" || buc_die "RBGJV steps directory not found: ${ZRBFC_RBGJV_STEPS_DIR}"
  readonly ZRBFC_RBGJR_STEPS_DIR="${z_self_dir}/rbgjr"
  test -d "${ZRBFC_RBGJR_STEPS_DIR}" || buc_die "RBGJR steps directory not found: ${ZRBFC_RBGJR_STEPS_DIR}"

  buc_log_args 'Tool image refs — mutable kindle state, populated by zrbfc_resolve_tool_images'
  z_rbfc_tool_gcloud=""
  z_rbfc_tool_docker=""
  z_rbfc_tool_alpine=""
  z_rbfc_tool_syft=""
  z_rbfc_tool_binfmt=""
  z_rbfc_tool_skopeo=""

  readonly ZRBFC_KINDLED=1
}

zrbfc_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBFC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbfc not kindled - call zrbfc_kindle first"
}

# Internal: Resolve tool image references from reliquary.
# Must be called after vessel load (reads RBRV_RELIQUARY).
# Sets module-level z_rbfc_tool_* mutable kindle state for downstream step assembly.
# Idempotent — safe to call multiple times per invocation.
zrbfc_resolve_tool_images() {
  zrbfc_sentinel

  local -r z_reliquary="${RBRV_RELIQUARY:-}"
  test -n "${z_reliquary}" \
    || buc_die "RBRV_RELIQUARY is required — run inscribe to create a reliquary first"

  local -r z_rqy_prefix="${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}/${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/${z_reliquary}"
  z_rbfc_tool_gcloud="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_GCLOUD}:${z_reliquary}"
  z_rbfc_tool_docker="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_DOCKER}:${z_reliquary}"
  z_rbfc_tool_alpine="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_ALPINE}:${z_reliquary}"
  z_rbfc_tool_syft="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SYFT}:${z_reliquary}"
  z_rbfc_tool_binfmt="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_BINFMT}:${z_reliquary}"
  z_rbfc_tool_skopeo="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SKOPEO}:${z_reliquary}"
  buc_log_args "Tool images resolved from reliquary: ${z_reliquary}"
}

# Validate that a vessel sigil is non-empty and corresponds to a known vessel.
# On missing or invalid sigil, lists available vessels and dies.
# Does NOT resolve to a directory — use zrbfc_resolve_vessel for that.
rbfc_require_vessel_sigil() {
  zrbfc_sentinel

  local -r z_sigil="${1:-}"

  if test -n "${z_sigil}" && test -d "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_sigil}" && test -f "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_sigil}/rbrv.env"; then
    return 0
  fi

  local z_sigils=""

rbfc_plumb_full() {
  zrbfc_sentinel

  local -r z_hallmark="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"

  buc_doc_brief "Plumb a hallmark's trust posture (full detail)"
  buc_doc_param "hallmark" "Full hallmark (e.g., c260305133650-r260305160530)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  zrbfc_plumb_core "${z_hallmark}" "full"
}

rbfc_plumb_compact() {
  zrbfc_sentinel

  local -r z_hallmark="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"

  buc_doc_brief "Plumb a hallmark's trust posture (compact summary)"
  buc_doc_param "hallmark" "Full hallmark (e.g., c260305133650-r260305160530)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  zrbfc_plumb_core "${z_hallmark}" "compact"
}

107	  test "${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE}" = "conjure" \
108	    || buc_die "Kludge only supports conjure vessels (got: ${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE})"
109	  test -n "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE:-}" \
110	    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' has no RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE"
111	  test -n "${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT:-}" \
112	    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' has no RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT"
113	  test -f "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}" \
114	    || buc_die "Dockerfile not found: ${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"
115	  test -d "${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}" \
116	    || buc_die "Build context not found: ${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}"
117	
118	  # Resolve base images — mirror conjure's anchor-aware resolution so an enshrined

120	  # Slot types diverge by credential need: anchored refs point into GAR and need
121	  # GCP auth, so misses refuse with a wrest remediation (credentialed-out-of-band).
122	  # Origin refs are public upstream and need no credentials, so misses auto-pull
123	  # inline. The presence guard's local-cache assertion is the tag-drift defense:
124	  # docker build (buildx default: no re-pull) runs against the cached image.
125	  local -r z_gar_repo_base="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
126	  local z_build_args=()

132	  local z_slot_ref=""
133	  local z_pkg_path=""
134	  local z_tag=""
135	  local z_miss=""
136	  local z_anchored_misses=()

139	    z_anchor_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_slot}_ANCHOR"
140	    z_origin="${!z_origin_var:-}"
141	    z_anchor="${!z_anchor_var:-}"
142	
143	    if test -z "${z_origin}" && test -n "${z_anchor}"; then
144	      buc_die "Malformed regime: ${z_anchor_var}=${z_anchor} set but ${z_origin_var} is empty"
145	    fi
146	    test -n "${z_origin}" || continue
147	
148	    if test -n "${z_anchor}"; then
149	      case "${z_anchor}" in
150	        *:*) : ;;
151	        *)   buc_die "Invalid ${z_anchor_var} locator format (expected package-path:tag): ${z_anchor}" ;;
152	      esac
153	      z_pkg_path="${z_anchor%:*}"
154	      z_tag="${z_anchor##*:}"
155	      test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
156	      test -n "${z_tag}"      || buc_die "Tag is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"

158	      buc_info "Image slot ${z_slot} (anchored): ${z_slot_ref}"

160	        || z_anchored_misses+=("${z_anchor}")

163	      buc_info "Image slot ${z_slot} (pass-through): ${z_slot_ref}"

165	        buc_info "Origin image not cached — pulling from upstream: ${z_slot_ref}"
166	        docker pull "${z_slot_ref}" \
167	          || buc_die "docker pull failed for origin slot ${z_slot}: ${z_slot_ref}"

169	          || buc_die "Origin image still absent from local cache after pull: ${z_slot_ref}"
170	      fi
171	    fi

173	    z_build_args+=("--build-arg" "RBF_IMAGE_${z_slot}=${z_slot_ref}")
174	  done
175	  (( ${#z_build_args[@]} )) || buc_die "No RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN found in vessel config"

177	  if (( ${#z_anchored_misses[@]} )); then
178	    buc_warn "Kludge cannot proceed — anchored base image(s) not cached locally"
179	    buc_bare "  Anchored slots point into GAR and require credentials. Kludge runs"

#
# Builds a vessel image locally using docker build, tags it with a
# kludge hallmark (k-prefixed timestamp) in the same GAR-style
# format that compose and rbob_charge expect. Also creates a fake
# vouch tag (same image, aliased) so the vouch gate passes.
#
# No Cloud Build, no GAR push, no credentials consumed.
# Host platform only (no multi-arch).

rbfk_kludge() {
  zrbfk_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Build vessel image locally for development (no Cloud Build, no GAR push)"
  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path to vessel directory"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Dirty-tree guard — kludge images must correspond to a committed state
  buc_step "Verifying clean working tree"
  git diff --quiet \
    || buc_die "Working tree has unstaged changes — commit before kludge"
  git diff --cached --quiet \
    || buc_die "Index has staged changes — commit before kludge"

  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path)
  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"

  # Load vessel configuration
  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"

  # Validate conjure mode (bind and graft don't have local Dockerfiles)
  test "${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE}" = "conjure" \
    || buc_die "Kludge only supports conjure vessels (got: ${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE})"
  test -n "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE:-}" \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' has no RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE"
  test -n "${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT:-}" \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' has no RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT"
  test -f "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}" \
    || buc_die "Dockerfile not found: ${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"
  test -d "${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}" \
    || buc_die "Build context not found: ${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}"

  # Resolve base images — mirror conjure's anchor-aware resolution so an enshrined
  # vessel built via kludge resolves the same GAR-anchored layers as conjure.
  # Slot types diverge by credential need: anchored refs point into GAR and need
  # GCP auth, so misses refuse with a wrest remediation (credentialed-out-of-band).
  # Origin refs are public upstream and need no credentials, so misses auto-pull
  # inline. The presence guard's local-cache assertion is the tag-drift defense:
  # docker build (buildx default: no re-pull) runs against the cached image.
  local -r z_gar_repo_base="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
  local z_build_args=()
  local z_slot=""
  local z_origin_var=""
  local z_anchor_var=""
  local z_origin=""
  local z_anchor=""
  local z_slot_ref=""
  local z_pkg_path=""
  local z_tag=""
  local z_miss=""
  local z_anchored_misses=()
  for z_slot in 1 2 3; do
    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_slot}_ORIGIN"
    z_anchor_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_slot}_ANCHOR"
    z_origin="${!z_origin_var:-}"
    z_anchor="${!z_anchor_var:-}"

    if test -z "${z_origin}" && test -n "${z_anchor}"; then
      buc_die "Malformed regime: ${z_anchor_var}=${z_anchor} set but ${z_origin_var} is empty"
    fi
    test -n "${z_origin}" || continue

    if test -n "${z_anchor}"; then
      case "${z_anchor}" in
        *:*) : ;;
        *)   buc_die "Invalid ${z_anchor_var} locator format (expected package-path:tag): ${z_anchor}" ;;
      esac
      z_pkg_path="${z_anchor%:*}"
      z_tag="${z_anchor##*:}"
      test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
      test -n "${z_tag}"      || buc_die "Tag is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
      z_slot_ref="${z_gar_repo_base}/${z_pkg_path}:${z_tag}"
      buc_info "Image slot ${z_slot} (anchored): ${z_slot_ref}"
      zrbfk_image_present_predicate "${z_slot_ref}" \
        || z_anchored_misses+=("${z_anchor}")
    else
      z_slot_ref="${z_origin}"
      buc_info "Image slot ${z_slot} (pass-through): ${z_slot_ref}"
      if ! zrbfk_image_present_predicate "${z_slot_ref}"; then
        buc_info "Origin image not cached — pulling from upstream: ${z_slot_ref}"
        docker pull "${z_slot_ref}" \
          || buc_die "docker pull failed for origin slot ${z_slot}: ${z_slot_ref}"
        zrbfk_image_present_predicate "${z_slot_ref}" \
          || buc_die "Origin image still absent from local cache after pull: ${z_slot_ref}"
      fi
    fi

    z_build_args+=("--build-arg" "RBF_IMAGE_${z_slot}=${z_slot_ref}")
  done
  (( ${#z_build_args[@]} )) || buc_die "No RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN found in vessel config"

  if (( ${#z_anchored_misses[@]} )); then
    buc_warn "Kludge cannot proceed — anchored base image(s) not cached locally"
    buc_bare "  Anchored slots point into GAR and require credentials. Kludge runs"
    buc_bare "  uncredentialed, so anchored images must be wrested into the local"
    buc_bare "  docker cache out-of-band before kludge runs."
    buc_bare ""
    buc_bare "  Anchored slots (wrest from GAR):"
    for z_miss in "${z_anchored_misses[@]}"; do
      buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_WREST_ENSHRINED_IMAGE}" "${z_miss}"
    done
    buc_bare ""
    buc_die "Local image cache incomplete — see remediation above"
  fi

  buc_step "Validating Dockerfile hygiene"
  rbfh_dockerfile_check "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"

  # Timestamp for chronological sorting, git describe for commit provenance

content.subject: Phase 1 — Mint rbfh_cli.sh + zipper entries

content.subject: Phase 2 — Theurge helper + dockerfile-hygiene fixture
content.description: Add rbtdrf_run_tt_neg helper in rbtdrf_fast.rs. Define 9-case dockerfile-hygiene fixture (8 synthetic + 1 all-vessels). Register fixture in rbtdrm_manifest.rs.

content.subject: Phase 3 — Wire fixture into ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST and ZRBTE_SUITE_GAUNTLET
content.description: Reformat ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST multiline; add dockerfile-hygiene there (propagates to service/crucible/complete via spread). Add explicit entry to ZRBTE_SUITE_GAUNTLET between regime-smoke and hallmark-lifecycle.

content.subject: Phase 4 — Touches + verification
content.description: Extend RBFH CLAUDE.md acronym entry with CLI parenthetical. Run tt/rbw-MG to regenerate tabtarget context. Run bash -n on touched bash, cargo build in Tools/rbk/rbtd, then tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh. Sanity-check verdict wiring with a flip+revert.

        return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
            "rbw-dU empty-arg refusal did not point at rbw-dl for operator discovery\n\
             stdout:\n{}\n\nstderr:\n{}",
            stdout, stderr
        ));
    }

    rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
}

54	  # case 1 is the entry-contract gate; preceding enrollment-validation runs

181	      local -r z_iterations=60

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# RBTE Engine - Theurge test engine implementation module
#
# BCG module providing kindle/sentinel/public functions for theurge
# Rust build, test, and orchestration commands.

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard
test -z "${ZRBTE_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZRBTE_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Kindle

zrbte_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBTE_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "rbte already kindled"

  local z_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"

  readonly RBTE_MANIFEST="${z_dir}/Cargo.toml"
  readonly ZRBTE_BINARY="${z_dir}/target/debug/rbtd"

  # Theurge-own colophons (routed by rbte_dispatch, not the RBK zipper)
  readonly ZRBTE_COLOPHONS="rbtd-ap"

  # Combined manifest: RBK zipper colophons + theurge-own colophons
  readonly ZRBTE_FULL_MANIFEST="${ZRBZ_COLOPHON_MANIFEST} ${ZRBTE_COLOPHONS}"

  # Suite-to-fixture mappings
  ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST=("enrollment-validation" "regime-validation" "regime-smoke" "handbook-render")
  ZRBTE_SUITE_SERVICE=("${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[@]}" "access-probe" "hallmark-lifecycle" "batch-vouch")
  ZRBTE_SUITE_CRUCIBLE=("${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[@]}" "tadmor" "srjcl" "pluml")
  ZRBTE_SUITE_COMPLETE=("${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[@]}" "access-probe" "hallmark-lifecycle" "batch-vouch" "tadmor" "srjcl" "pluml")
  # Gauntlet — release-qualification ladder, walks marshal-zero state through
  # canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. Pristine-lifecycle
  # case 1 is the entry-contract gate; preceding enrollment-validation runs
  # state-indifferent and is harmless on broken state. Fail-fast across
  # fixtures is provided by the for-loop's set -e.
  ZRBTE_SUITE_GAUNTLET=(
    "enrollment-validation"
    "pristine-lifecycle"
    "canonical-establish"
    "onboarding-sequence"
    "regime-validation"
    "regime-smoke"
    "hallmark-lifecycle"
    "tadmor"
    "moriah"
    "srjcl"
    "pluml"
  )

  readonly ZRBTE_KINDLED=1
}

######################################################################
# Sentinel

zrbte_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBTE_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbte not kindled - call zrbte_kindle first"
}

######################################################################
# Internal helpers

zrbte_build_binary() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  buc_step "Building theurge"
  cargo build --manifest-path "${RBTE_MANIFEST}" || buc_die "cargo build failed"
  test -x "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" || buc_die "Theurge binary not found: ${ZRBTE_BINARY}"
}

zrbte_resolve_suite() {
  local z_suite="$1"
  case "${z_suite}" in
    fast)     echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[*]}" ;;
    service)  echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_SERVICE[*]}" ;;
    crucible) echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_CRUCIBLE[*]}" ;;
    complete) echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_COMPLETE[*]}" ;;
    gauntlet) echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_GAUNTLET[*]}" ;;
    *)        buc_die "Unknown suite: ${z_suite} (expected fast|service|crucible|complete|gauntlet)" ;;
  esac
}

######################################################################
# Public functions

rbte_build() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  buc_step "Building theurge"
  buc_log_args "Manifest: ${RBTE_MANIFEST}"
  cargo build --manifest-path "${RBTE_MANIFEST}" "$@" || buc_die "cargo build failed"
  buc_success "Theurge built"
}

rbte_test() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  buc_step "Testing theurge"
  buc_log_args "Manifest: ${RBTE_MANIFEST}"
  cargo test --manifest-path "${RBTE_MANIFEST}" "$@" || buc_die "cargo test failed"
  buc_success "All theurge tests passed"
}

rbte_run() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  local z_fixture="${BURD_TOKEN_3:-}"
  test -n "${z_fixture}" || buc_die "No fixture imprint — use tabtarget with imprint (e.g. rbtd-r.FixtureRun.tadmor.sh)"

  zrbte_build_binary

  buc_step "Running theurge fixture '${z_fixture}'"
  "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" "${ZRBTE_FULL_MANIFEST}" "${z_fixture}"
}

rbte_suite() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  local z_suite="${BURD_TOKEN_3:-}"
  test -n "${z_suite}" || buc_die "No suite imprint — use tabtarget with imprint (e.g. rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh)"

  local z_fixture_list
  z_fixture_list="$(zrbte_resolve_suite "${z_suite}")"

  zrbte_build_binary

  local z_count=0
  local z_fixture
  for z_fixture in ${z_fixture_list}; do
    buc_step "Running theurge fixture '${z_fixture}'"
    "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" "${ZRBTE_FULL_MANIFEST}" "${z_fixture}"
    z_count=$((z_count + 1))
  done

  buc_success "Suite '${z_suite}' complete (${z_count} fixtures)"
}

rbte_single() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  zrbte_build_binary

  local z_fixture="${1:-}"
  local z_case="${2:-}"
  "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" single "${ZRBTE_FULL_MANIFEST}" ${z_fixture:+"${z_fixture}"} ${z_case:+"${z_case}"}
}

rbte_probe() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  local -r z_role="${BURD_TOKEN_3:-}"
  test -n "${z_role}" || buc_die "No role imprint — use tabtarget with imprint (e.g. rbtd-ap.AccessProbe.governor.sh)"

  case "${z_role}" in
    governor|director|retriever)
      # JWT SA propagation-absorbing budget: 60 attempts × 3000ms ≈ 180s worst case.
      # Happy path exits on first 2xx; budget consumed only when post-mint IAM
      # propagation lags. Each retry mints a fresh JWT-OAuth token, so the inner
      # rbgo_get_token_capture race (BBAAp) is exercised independently per attempt.
      local -r z_iterations=60
      local -r z_delay_ms=3000
      buc_step "JWT SA access probe: ${z_role}"
      rbgv_jwt_sa_probe "${z_role}" "${z_iterations}" "${z_delay_ms}"
      buc_success "${z_role} JWT access probe passed"
      ;;
    payor)
      # Payor probe semantic unchanged: stability-sample loop.
      local -r z_iterations=5
      local -r z_delay_ms=1500
      buc_step "Payor OAuth access probe"
      source "${RBBC_rbrp_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source RBRP: ${RBBC_rbrp_file}"
      zrbrp_kindle
      zrbrp_enforce
      rbgv_payor_oauth_probe "${z_iterations}" "${z_delay_ms}"
      buc_success "Payor OAuth access probe passed"
      ;;
    *)
      buc_die "Unknown access-probe role: ${z_role} (expected governor|director|retriever|payor)"
      ;;
  esac
}

# eof

ZRBFK_SOURCED=1

# Source shared Foundry Core module (registry constants + vessel resolution helpers)
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfc_FoundryCore.sh"

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbfk_*)

zrbfk_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBFK_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfk already kindled"

  buc_log_args 'Kindle shared Foundry Core infrastructure'
  zrbfc_kindle

  buc_log_args 'Define forensic temp prefix for docker image inspect stderr capture'
  readonly ZRBFK_INSPECT_STDERR_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfk_inspect_stderr_"

  buc_log_args 'Initialize mutable image-presence inspect counter'
  z_rbfk_inspect_counter=0

  readonly ZRBFK_KINDLED=1
}

zrbfk_sentinel() {
  zrbfc_sentinel
  test "${ZRBFK_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbfk not kindled - call zrbfk_kindle first"
}

# Image-Presence Predicate
#
# Wraps `docker image inspect` so stderr lands in a counter-discriminated
# forensic temp file rather than the terminal. Returns docker's exit
# status; callers consume via `if zrbfk_image_present_predicate ...` or
# `... || ...`. The forensic files persist in BURD_TEMP_DIR for the
# command's lifetime, available for post-mortem inspection.
zrbfk_image_present_predicate() {
  zrbfk_sentinel
  local -r z_ref="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_ref}" || buc_die "zrbfk_image_present_predicate: image ref required"
  z_rbfk_inspect_counter=$(( z_rbfk_inspect_counter + 1 ))
  docker image inspect "${z_ref}" \
    >/dev/null \
    2>"${ZRBFK_INSPECT_STDERR_PREFIX}${z_rbfk_inspect_counter}"
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (rbfk_*)

######################################################################
# Kludge Build - Local image build for development
#
# Builds a vessel image locally using docker build, tags it with a
# kludge hallmark (k-prefixed timestamp) in the same GAR-style
# format that compose and rbob_charge expect. Also creates a fake
# vouch tag (same image, aliased) so the vouch gate passes.
#
# No Cloud Build, no GAR push, no credentials consumed.
# Host platform only (no multi-arch).

rbfk_kludge() {
  zrbfk_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Build vessel image locally for development (no Cloud Build, no GAR push)"
  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path to vessel directory"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Dirty-tree guard — kludge images must correspond to a committed state
  buc_step "Verifying clean working tree"
  git diff --quiet \
    || buc_die "Working tree has unstaged changes — commit before kludge"
  git diff --cached --quiet \
    || buc_die "Index has staged changes — commit before kludge"

  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path)
  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# RBFH CLI - Recipe Bottle Foundry Hygiene command-line interface
#
# Surfaces the Dockerfile FROM-line constraint enforced by the rbfh
# library module so that theurge fixtures (and operators) can drive
# the contract from outside the kludge/conjure code paths.
#
# Commands:
#   check         Check a Dockerfile path for FROM-line hygiene
#   check_vessel  Check a vessel's RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE for hygiene

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

######################################################################
# CLI Commands

rbfh_check() {
  zrbfh_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Check a Dockerfile against the FROM-line hygiene contract"
  buc_doc_param "dockerfile" "Path to the Dockerfile to check"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local -r z_path="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  test -n "${z_path}" || buc_die "Dockerfile path required (free-form path argument)"

  buc_step "RBFH Check: ${z_path}"
  rbfh_dockerfile_check "${z_path}"
  buc_success "Dockerfile passes hygiene contract: ${z_path}"
}

rbfh_check_vessel() {
  zrbfh_sentinel
  zrbfc_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Check a vessel's conjure Dockerfile against the FROM-line hygiene contract"
  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path to vessel directory"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path) — lists-and-dies on missing/invalid
  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"

  # Source the vessel's rbrv.env to pick up RBRV_VESSEL_MODE and RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE
  source "${z_vessel_dir}/rbrv.env" || buc_die "Failed to source vessel rbrv.env: ${z_vessel_dir}/rbrv.env"

  # Hygiene applies to local Dockerfiles; bind/graft vessels have no Dockerfile to check
  test "${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE:-}" = "conjure" \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL:-${z_vessel_dir}}' is mode '${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE:-<unset>}' — hygiene check applies only to conjure vessels"

  test -n "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE:-}" \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL:-${z_vessel_dir}}' has no RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE"

  buc_step "RBFH Check Vessel: ${RBRV_SIGIL:-${z_vessel_dir}} → ${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"
  rbfh_dockerfile_check "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"
  buc_success "Vessel Dockerfile passes hygiene contract: ${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"
}

######################################################################
# Furnish and Main

zrbfh_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TOOLS_DIR        " "Project tools root directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TEMP_DIR         " "Bash Dispatch Utility provided temporary directory, empty at start of command"
  buc_doc_env "BUZ_FOLIO             " "Dockerfile path (rbfh_check) or vessel sigil/path (rbfh_check_vessel)"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  source "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/rbbc_constants.sh"
  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbcc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgl_GarLayout.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgd_DepotConstants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrr_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrv_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh"
  source "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgo_OAuth.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbfc_FoundryCore.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbz_zipper.sh"

  zbuv_kindle
  zburd_kindle
  zrbcc_kindle

  zrbrr_kindle
  zrbrr_enforce
  zrbdc_kindle

  zrbgc_kindle
  zrbgl_kindle
  zrbgd_kindle
  zrbgo_kindle
  zrbfc_kindle
  zrbfh_kindle

  zbuz_kindle
  zrbz_kindle
}

buc_execute rbfh_ "Recipe Bottle Foundry Hygiene" zrbfh_furnish "$@"

# eof

######################################################################
# Command: generate - Generate tabtarget context markdown from zipper registry

rblm_generate() {
  buc_doc_brief "Generate tabtarget context markdown from zipper registry"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local -r z_target="${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}/rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md"
  local -r z_tmp="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md"

  buz_emit_context "${BURD_TABTARGET_DIR}" > "${z_tmp}" || buc_die "Failed to generate context"

  if test -f "${z_target}" && diff -q "${z_tmp}" "${z_target}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    buc_log_args "Context file already up to date: ${z_target}"
  else
    cp "${z_tmp}" "${z_target}" || buc_die "Failed to write: ${z_target}"
    buc_log_args "Generated: ${z_target}"
  fi

  buc_success "Context generation complete"
}

######################################################################
# Furnish and Main

zrblm_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TOOLS_DIR        " "Project tools root directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TEMP_DIR         " "Temporary directory for this invocation (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TABTARGET_DIR    " "Tabtarget directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  source "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/rbbc_constants.sh"
  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbcc_Constants.sh" || buc_die "Failed to source rbcc_Constants.sh"

  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"         || buc_die "Failed to source buym_yelp.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buh_handbook.sh"      || buc_die "Failed to source buh_handbook.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"     || buc_die "Failed to source buz_zipper.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbz_zipper.sh"    || buc_die "Failed to source rbz_zipper.sh"
  zbuz_kindle
  zrbz_kindle
}

buc_execute rblm_ "Lifecycle Marshal" zrblm_furnish "$@"

# eof

2352	/// rbtdrc_lookup_fixture and the helpful "list valid fixtures" diagnostic the
2353	/// single-case tabtarget emits on missing/unknown fixture arg. Declaration
2354	/// order is also the listing order operators see.

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# RBTE Engine - Theurge test engine implementation module
#
# BCG module providing kindle/sentinel/public functions for theurge
# Rust build, test, and orchestration commands.

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard
test -z "${ZRBTE_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZRBTE_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Kindle

zrbte_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBTE_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "rbte already kindled"

  local z_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"

  readonly RBTE_MANIFEST="${z_dir}/Cargo.toml"
  readonly ZRBTE_BINARY="${z_dir}/target/debug/rbtd"

  # Theurge-own colophons (routed by rbte_dispatch, not the RBK zipper)
  readonly ZRBTE_COLOPHONS="rbtd-ap"

  # Combined manifest: RBK zipper colophons + theurge-own colophons
  readonly ZRBTE_FULL_MANIFEST="${ZRBZ_COLOPHON_MANIFEST} ${ZRBTE_COLOPHONS}"

  # Suite-to-fixture mappings
  ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST=("enrollment-validation" "regime-validation" "regime-smoke" "handbook-render")
  ZRBTE_SUITE_SERVICE=("${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[@]}" "access-probe" "hallmark-lifecycle" "batch-vouch")
  ZRBTE_SUITE_CRUCIBLE=("${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[@]}" "tadmor" "srjcl" "pluml")
  ZRBTE_SUITE_COMPLETE=("${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[@]}" "access-probe" "hallmark-lifecycle" "batch-vouch" "tadmor" "srjcl" "pluml")
  # Gauntlet — release-qualification ladder, walks marshal-zero state through
  # canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. Pristine-lifecycle
  # case 1 is the entry-contract gate; preceding enrollment-validation runs
  # state-indifferent and is harmless on broken state. Fail-fast across
  # fixtures is provided by the for-loop's set -e.
  ZRBTE_SUITE_GAUNTLET=(
    "enrollment-validation"
    "pristine-lifecycle"
    "canonical-establish"
    "onboarding-sequence"
    "regime-validation"
    "regime-smoke"
    "hallmark-lifecycle"
    "tadmor"
    "moriah"
    "srjcl"
    "pluml"
  )

  readonly ZRBTE_KINDLED=1
}

######################################################################
# Sentinel

zrbte_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBTE_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbte not kindled - call zrbte_kindle first"
}

######################################################################
# Internal helpers

zrbte_build_binary() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  buc_step "Building theurge"
  cargo build --manifest-path "${RBTE_MANIFEST}" || buc_die "cargo build failed"
  test -x "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" || buc_die "Theurge binary not found: ${ZRBTE_BINARY}"
}

zrbte_resolve_suite() {
  local z_suite="$1"
  case "${z_suite}" in
    fast)     echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[*]}" ;;
    service)  echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_SERVICE[*]}" ;;
    crucible) echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_CRUCIBLE[*]}" ;;
    complete) echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_COMPLETE[*]}" ;;
    gauntlet) echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_GAUNTLET[*]}" ;;
    *)        buc_die "Unknown suite: ${z_suite} (expected fast|service|crucible|complete|gauntlet)" ;;
  esac
}

######################################################################
# Public functions

rbte_build() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  buc_step "Building theurge"
  buc_log_args "Manifest: ${RBTE_MANIFEST}"
  cargo build --manifest-path "${RBTE_MANIFEST}" "$@" || buc_die "cargo build failed"
  buc_success "Theurge built"
}

rbte_test() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  buc_step "Testing theurge"
  buc_log_args "Manifest: ${RBTE_MANIFEST}"
  cargo test --manifest-path "${RBTE_MANIFEST}" "$@" || buc_die "cargo test failed"
  buc_success "All theurge tests passed"
}

rbte_run() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  local z_fixture="${BURD_TOKEN_3:-}"
  test -n "${z_fixture}" || buc_die "No fixture imprint — use tabtarget with imprint (e.g. rbtd-r.FixtureRun.tadmor.sh)"

  zrbte_build_binary

  buc_step "Running theurge fixture '${z_fixture}'"
  "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" "${ZRBTE_FULL_MANIFEST}" "${z_fixture}"
}

rbte_suite() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  local z_suite="${BURD_TOKEN_3:-}"
  test -n "${z_suite}" || buc_die "No suite imprint — use tabtarget with imprint (e.g. rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh)"

  local z_fixture_list
  z_fixture_list="$(zrbte_resolve_suite "${z_suite}")"

  zrbte_build_binary

  local z_count=0
  local z_fixture
  for z_fixture in ${z_fixture_list}; do
    buc_step "Running theurge fixture '${z_fixture}'"
    "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" "${ZRBTE_FULL_MANIFEST}" "${z_fixture}"
    z_count=$((z_count + 1))
  done

  buc_success "Suite '${z_suite}' complete (${z_count} fixtures)"
}

rbte_single() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  zrbte_build_binary

  local z_fixture="${1:-}"
  local z_case="${2:-}"
  "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" single "${ZRBTE_FULL_MANIFEST}" ${z_fixture:+"${z_fixture}"} ${z_case:+"${z_case}"}
}

rbte_probe() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  local -r z_role="${BURD_TOKEN_3:-}"
  test -n "${z_role}" || buc_die "No role imprint — use tabtarget with imprint (e.g. rbtd-ap.AccessProbe.governor.sh)"

  case "${z_role}" in
    governor|director|retriever)
      # JWT SA propagation-absorbing budget: 60 attempts × 3000ms ≈ 180s worst case.
      # Happy path exits on first 2xx; budget consumed only when post-mint IAM
      # propagation lags. Each retry mints a fresh JWT-OAuth token, so the inner
      # rbgo_get_token_capture race (BBAAp) is exercised independently per attempt.
      local -r z_iterations=60
      local -r z_delay_ms=3000
      buc_step "JWT SA access probe: ${z_role}"
      rbgv_jwt_sa_probe "${z_role}" "${z_iterations}" "${z_delay_ms}"
      buc_success "${z_role} JWT access probe passed"
      ;;
    payor)
      # Payor probe semantic unchanged: stability-sample loop.
      local -r z_iterations=5
      local -r z_delay_ms=1500
      buc_step "Payor OAuth access probe"
      source "${RBBC_rbrp_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source RBRP: ${RBBC_rbrp_file}"
      zrbrp_kindle
      zrbrp_enforce
      rbgv_payor_oauth_probe "${z_iterations}" "${z_delay_ms}"
      buc_success "Payor OAuth access probe passed"
      ;;
    *)
      buc_die "Unknown access-probe role: ${z_role} (expected governor|director|retriever|payor)"
      ;;
  esac
}

# eof

content.subject: Phase 1 — Mint rbfh_cli.sh + zipper entries
content.description: Create Tools/rbk/rbfh_cli.sh with rbfh_check and rbfh_check_vessel functions. Add RBZ_HYGIENE_CHECK and RBZ_HYGIENE_CHECK_VESSEL zipper entries in rbz_zipper.sh under Foundry group.

content.subject: Phase 2 — Theurge helper + dockerfile-hygiene fixture
content.description: Add rbtdrf_run_tt_neg helper in rbtdrf_fast.rs. Define 9-case dockerfile-hygiene fixture (8 synthetic + 1 all-vessels). Register fixture in rbtdrm_manifest.rs.

content.subject: Phase 3 — Wire fixture into ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST and ZRBTE_SUITE_GAUNTLET
content.description: Reformat ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST multiline; add dockerfile-hygiene there (propagates to service/crucible/complete via spread). Add explicit entry to ZRBTE_SUITE_GAUNTLET between regime-smoke and hallmark-lifecycle.

content.subject: Phase 4 — Touches + verification
content.description: Extend RBFH CLAUDE.md acronym entry with CLI parenthetical. Run tt/rbw-MG to regenerate tabtarget context. Run bash -n on touched bash, cargo build in Tools/rbk/rbtd, then tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh. Sanity-check verdict wiring with a flip+revert.

All four phases done. Summary of what landed for `₢BBABJ`:

**Phase 1 (CLI + tabtargets)**

**Phase 2 (theurge fixture)**

lastPrompt: notch now.  Then help me understand somethign that concerns me: I saw your edit below and am not terribly pleased to see bash environment 'alternatives' where you are setting environment variables dir…

content.text:
Committed as `e6e2a2e4`.

Now your concern. **You're right to flag this.** Here's an honest read:

## The contract leak

Look at what `rbtdrf_dh_all_vessels_pass` actually does:

1. Resolve `RBRR_VESSEL_DIR` via a one-shot kindle ceremony — necessary, no tabtarget surfaces this value.
2. `read_dir(vessel_dir)` to enumerate entries — bypasses any tabtarget enumeration.
3. For each entry, source `rbrv.env` directly via bash and grep `RBRV_VESSEL_MODE` — **bypasses the tabtarget surface to read module-internal regime fields.**
4. If conjure, invoke `rbw-fhv` — this part is clean.

Step 3 is the leak. The docket explicitly said *"Theurge knows colophons and sigils; not source paths or kindle chains."* My implementation knows:
- `RBRR_VESSEL_DIR` — leaked from rbrr internals (defensible — needed for enumeration; no listing tabtarget).
- The path layout `<vessel-dir>/rbrv.env` — leaked from rbrv conventions.
- That sourcing rbrv.env exposes `RBRV_VESSEL_MODE` — leaked from regime field naming.
- That rbrv.env interpolates `${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}` and breaks under `set -u` if it isn't exported — exactly the brittleness my "fix" papers over.

The env-export wasn't an architectural decision. It was a workaround for a contract that was already torn open. The real problem is that I went into rbrv.env at all.

## Why this matters beyond aesthetics

The discipline behind theurge is that the test surface and the operator surface are the same. When operators add a new `RBRR_*` field that rbrv.env interpolates, **operators discover it instantly through the tabtargets** — but theurge tests using direct `source` only discover it through `set -u` regressions. That's exactly the inversion theurge exists to prevent.

It also makes the test a regression magnet. The pre-existing `rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels` failure I tripped over today is the same class of bug — it sources rbrv.env directly and dies on `RBRV_RELIQUARY` unbound. My new case is sitting one regime-field-rename away from joining it.

## Cleaner approaches

In ascending order of investment:

**A. Distinguished exit code from `rbw-fhv` for non-conjure (recommended).** Modify `rbfh_check_vessel` to exit with a specific code (say `2`) when the vessel is non-conjure, while keeping the human message. Theurge enumerates vessels (via `rbw-rvl` listing), invokes `rbw-fhv` on each, treats exit `2` as silent skip and counts only exit `0`. **Theurge stops touching rbrv.env entirely.** Operator UX unchanged — direct invocation still surfaces the operator-actionable error.

**B. New iteration tabtarget (e.g., `rbw-fhV.HygieneCheckAllVessels.sh`).** Encapsulates "iterate vessels, filter conjure, check each, fail-fast." Theurge invokes once, asserts exit 0. Cleanest from theurge's perspective; adds one more tabtarget surface but a meaningful one (operators get a one-shot "is the whole fleet hygienic?" check).

**C. Use existing `rbw-rvl` for enumeration, push mode-filter into rbw-fhv as in (A).** Combination — `rbw-rvl` becomes the official enumeration surface; theurge stops using `read_dir` on `RBRR_VESSEL_DIR` at all.

## The broader principle

The litmus test I should have applied before reaching for the env-var hack: *"Could an operator at the shell achieve this without sourcing internal regime files?"* If no, theurge needs a tabtarget for it — mint one rather than bypass. The rule isn't "no env vars in test scripts" — it's "no peeking past the surface contract."

My recommendation: chivvy a follow-up pace (or itch) to refactor `dh_all_vessels_pass` per option A — it's small (add the exit-2 contract to `rbfh_check_vessel`, simplify the Rust loop). The current implementation is functional but is a regression liability and contradicts the docket's own discipline statement.

Want me to slate that as a cantle pace right now (executes immediately after BBABJ wrap), or capture it as an itch?

content: Mounted ₢BBABJ (rbfh CLI + 9-case dockerfile-hygiene fixture) is implemented, tested green, and notched as e6e2a2e4. Next action: decide whether to slate a follow-up pace refactoring the all-vessels case off direct rbrv.env sourcing, or capture it as an itch. (disable recaps in /config)

message.content: I'm not quite sure that I understand.  Is the concern here that each failure path via the tabtarget be positively recognized by the test case for precise diagnosis of the issue?

content.text:
No — that's a related but distinct concern. Let me separate them:

**Your interpretation (diagnostic precision):** "When the test fails, the test knows exactly which contract failed and emits a precise diagnostic." This is about the *output* of a failed test.

**My actual concern (surface integrity):** "The test only interacts with the system through the same surfaces an operator does." This is about the *path* the test takes to do its work, regardless of pass or fail.

In my `dh_all_vessels_pass` implementation, the actual hygiene check goes through `rbw-fhv` (good — surface-respecting), but the *filter step* — "is this vessel conjure-mode?" — sources `rbrv.env` directly in raw bash. There's no tabtarget that answers "what mode is this vessel?", so the test reaches around the surface and reads the config file itself.

The cost isn't poor diagnostics. The cost is:

1. **The test now depends on rbrv.env's internal shape.** When someone adds a new `RBRR_*` interpolation, the test breaks (`set -u` + unbound var) even though no operator-visible behavior changed. The pre-existing `rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels` failure on main is exactly this — a regime-internal change broke a test that bypasses the surface.

2. **The "what does an operator see?" question diverges from "what does the test see?"** An operator running `rbw-fhv rbev-graft-demo` gets a clean conjure-mode error. The test never invokes that path — it pre-filters with bash and never asks the tabtarget. So the conjure-mode refusal contract is never exercised by the test, even though it's part of the surface I just minted.

The two concerns connect at the symmetric clean-up: distinguished exit codes from `rbw-fhv` (e.g., exit `2` for non-conjure) would simultaneously (a) let theurge stop sourcing rbrv.env (surface integrity) AND (b) give the test a precise signal to count vs. skip vs. fail (diagnostic precision). But the fundamental concern is integrity — diagnostic precision is a side benefit.

Sharper framing: **"Can an operator at a shell do everything this test does without sourcing module-internal files?"** If no, the test is using privileged access the operator doesn't have, and the system has no tabtarget surface for that capability — which is itself a finding.

Does that distinction land?

message.content: I might be limping towards the understanding... Would it be simpler for the 'verification tabtarget' to simply return success if the vessel is in a mode (graft?) that the dockerfile check is unnecessary?

content.text:
Yes — and that's actually cleaner than any of the three options I floated. Let me think through it as you've framed it.

## What changes

`rbfh_check_vessel` on a non-conjure vessel currently dies with "hygiene check applies only to conjure vessels." Change it to silently exit 0 (maybe a one-line `buc_info "skipping non-conjure vessel"` for transparency).

Justification: the hygiene contract is a property of the FROM-line. Graft vessels have no local FROM-line. The honest answer to "does this vessel pass FROM-line hygiene?" is **yes, vacuously** — there's nothing to violate. Not "you asked the wrong question."

## Why this is better than my distinguished-exit-codes proposal

I was treating "non-conjure" as a state the test needs to *recognize* — hence exit-2 to signal "skip." But the user's framing reveals that as overcomplicated: if non-conjure means "vacuously hygienic," the test doesn't need to recognize anything. It just invokes `rbw-fhv` on every vessel and asserts all exit 0.

Under that contract:
- **Theurge stops touching `rbrv.env` entirely.** Iteration becomes `read_dir(RBRR_VESSEL_DIR)` + invoke `rbw-fhv <sigil>` per entry. No mode lookup, no env-var hack, no second one-shot bash, no `set -u` brittleness when rbrv.env adds a new interpolation.
- **The surface stays minimal.** No new exit codes, no new tabtarget, no `--strict` flag — just the existing `rbfh_check_vessel` behavior softened on one branch.
- **Operator UX stays defensible.** An operator running `rbw-fhv rbev-graft-demo` gets exit 0 with maybe a single-line "non-conjure, nothing to check." If the operator typoed and meant a conjure vessel, the cost is "I got success on a vessel I wasn't expecting" — but `zrbfc_resolve_vessel` already protects against the more dangerous typo (entirely wrong sigil).

## What it costs

The "operator-actionable refusal on bind/graft" the docket called for goes away. That's a docket prescription I should re-examine rather than defend — it was written before the all-vessels iteration use-case landed, and the iteration use-case reveals the prescription was overspecified. The mid-execution posture from CLAUDE.md is: when implementation reveals a prescribed shape doesn't fit, surface mechanism + repair. That's what this is.

## One small caveat

The docket called for a "silent-zero-iteration trap" — fail if zero conjure vessels iterated. Under the new contract we can't (cheaply) distinguish "iterated N vessels, all vacuously" from "iterated N vessels, all really checked." I'd just **drop that specific trap** and replace it with "fail if zero vessels iterated at all" (catches a busted RBRR_VESSEL_DIR). The synthetic cases (`dh_accept_parameterized` etc.) already prove the check itself fires correctly; the all-vessels case becomes "all real vessels are clean," which is the regression property we actually want.

## Recommendation

Apply this now (small change — soften one branch in `rbfh_check_vessel`, strip mode-lookup from `dh_all_vessels_pass`, retitle the trap), or capture as a follow-up cantled pace? It's small enough I'd just do it now if you agree, then re-notch.

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# RBFH CLI - Recipe Bottle Foundry Hygiene command-line interface
#
# Surfaces the Dockerfile FROM-line constraint enforced by the rbfh
# library module so that theurge fixtures (and operators) can drive
# the contract from outside the kludge/conjure code paths.
#
# Commands:
#   check         Check a Dockerfile path for FROM-line hygiene
#   check_vessel  Check a vessel's RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE for hygiene

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

######################################################################
# CLI Commands

rbfh_check() {
  zrbfh_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Check a Dockerfile against the FROM-line hygiene contract"
  buc_doc_param "dockerfile" "Path to the Dockerfile to check"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local -r z_path="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  test -n "${z_path}" || buc_die "Dockerfile path required (free-form path argument)"

  buc_step "RBFH Check: ${z_path}"
  rbfh_dockerfile_check "${z_path}"
  buc_success "Dockerfile passes hygiene contract: ${z_path}"
}

rbfh_check_vessel() {
  zrbfh_sentinel
  zrbfc_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Check a vessel's conjure Dockerfile against the FROM-line hygiene contract"
  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path to vessel directory"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path) — lists-and-dies on missing/invalid
  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"

  # Source the vessel's rbrv.env to pick up RBRV_VESSEL_MODE and RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE
  source "${z_vessel_dir}/rbrv.env" || buc_die "Failed to source vessel rbrv.env: ${z_vessel_dir}/rbrv.env"

  # Hygiene applies to local Dockerfiles; bind/graft vessels have no Dockerfile to check
  test "${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE:-}" = "conjure" \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL:-${z_vessel_dir}}' is mode '${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE:-<unset>}' — hygiene check applies only to conjure vessels"

  test -n "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE:-}" \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL:-${z_vessel_dir}}' has no RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE"

  buc_step "RBFH Check Vessel: ${RBRV_SIGIL:-${z_vessel_dir}} → ${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"
  rbfh_dockerfile_check "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"
  buc_success "Vessel Dockerfile passes hygiene contract: ${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"
}

######################################################################
# Furnish and Main

zrbfh_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TOOLS_DIR        " "Project tools root directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TEMP_DIR         " "Bash Dispatch Utility provided temporary directory, empty at start of command"
  buc_doc_env "BUZ_FOLIO             " "Dockerfile path (rbfh_check) or vessel sigil/path (rbfh_check_vessel)"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  source "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/rbbc_constants.sh"
  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbcc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgl_GarLayout.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgd_DepotConstants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrr_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrv_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh"
  source "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgo_OAuth.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbfc_FoundryCore.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbz_zipper.sh"

  zbuv_kindle
  zburd_kindle
  zrbcc_kindle

  zrbrr_kindle
  zrbrr_enforce
  zrbdc_kindle

  zrbgc_kindle
  zrbgl_kindle
  zrbgd_kindle
  zrbgo_kindle
  zrbfc_kindle
  zrbfh_kindle

  zbuz_kindle
  zrbz_kindle
}

buc_execute rbfh_ "Recipe Bottle Foundry Hygiene" zrbfh_furnish "$@"

# eof
